context: Tests whether the agent follows a test-first development workflow: writing the spec before the implementation, documenting the expected failure before any service code is written, and explicitly planning the implementation scope before coding it.

checklist.name: process_log.md exists
checklist.description: A process_log.md file is present in the workspace

checklist.name: Spec content before implementation
checklist.description: In process_log.md, the section describing or quoting spec/test content appears earlier in the document than the section describing or quoting implementation code

checklist.name: Expected failure documented
checklist.description: process_log.md includes a description of what the test would produce (fail, error, or expected failure output) before implementation code was written — not just 'test passes'

checklist.name: Test design phase present
checklist.description: process_log.md contains a distinct phase or section focused on test design — describing the boundary being tested, edge cases, or what the spec is intended to verify

checklist.name: Implementation plan before code
checklist.description: process_log.md contains an explicit step where the scope of the implementation is planned (which class/methods will be created) before source code is written

checklist.name: No implementation code before spec phase
checklist.description: process_log.md does NOT show Ruby service implementation code in a section that precedes any spec/test content — implementation code only appears after test content

checklist.name: At least three distinct phases
checklist.description: process_log.md contains at least three clearly labelled or structurally distinct phases of development work

checklist.name: Spec uses describe/context structure
checklist.description: The spec file uses RSpec.describe for the class and at least one context block for a scenario variation
